metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC2 — e.g., `gh run list --branch main` after a commit to main to confirm test/eval/optionality still trigger, and a YAML inspection step confirming gerrit-verify.yml uses only `workflow_dispatch` (no `push:` block affected by the branches-ignore change).
findings.finding: AC2 ('main + PR triggers unchanged; gerrit-verify Verified path unaffected') has no concrete proving command or described step — an agent cannot confirm this criterion is met without guessing at a verification approach.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC2: 'main + PR triggers unchanged; gerrit-verify Verified path unaffected'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Evidence: (a) after merging, push a commit to main and confirm `gh run list --branch main` shows test/eval/optionality runs; (b) inspect gerrit-verify.yml and confirm it contains no `push: branches` trigger that would be suppressed — or trigger a synthetic gerrit-verify dispatch and observe the Verified vote cast on Gerrit.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Merge AC3 into AC1 (or remove AC3) since both criteria verify the same post-condition — zero push-triggered runs for feature/** — via the same evidence mechanism.
findings.finding: AC1 and AC3 are near-duplicate requirements: AC1 states 'test/eval/optionality no longer run on pushes to refs/heads/feature/**' and AC3 states 'A feature/** branch push produces zero push-triggered mirror runs' — both verify the same observable outcome via the same mechanism (gh run list), with AC3 adding no additional coverage.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove AC3 and expand AC1's evidence note to include the gh run list verification step already present in AC3.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback note (e.g., 'revert the branches-ignore lines via git revert <sha> on the mirror repo') so the back-out path is documented and not left implicit.
findings.finding: The plan has no explicit rollback/back-out plan for the workflow trigger change. T4 requires a reversibility mechanism beyond acknowledgment, yet none is stated.
findings.location: What section / Acceptance Criteria — no rollback section present
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line rollback note under What: 'Rollback: git revert the branches-ignore addition on the mirror; no data or state is mutated so rollback is immediate.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a comment block in each modified workflow file (test.yml, eval.yml, optionality.yml) or a CLAUDE.md/design-doc entry explaining that `branches-ignore: ['feature/**']` is intentional — it prevents CI fan-out from mirror replication of feature branches (epic 88ab S3), which carry no Verified vote weight.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a non-obvious, intentional divergence in CI trigger scope (feature/** excluded from push triggers on the mirror) with no mention of capturing this decision in a design doc, ADR, or CLAUDE.md. Future maintainers editing test.yml, eval.yml, or optionality.yml may not understand why feature/** is excluded and could inadvertently revert it.
findings.suggested_fix:
In each workflow file, directly above the `branches-ignore` stanza, add:
```yaml
# feature/** branches are excluded intentionally: they are replicated from Gerrit
# (epic 88ab S3) so g2p workflow_dispatch can resolve them, but push-triggered
# CI here is pure noise — Verified votes come solely from gerrit-verify.
# See: <link-to-ADR-or-design-doc>
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an end-state-observable verification step to AC2: e.g., 'push a commit to main and confirm `gh run list --branch main` shows test/eval/optionality runs; and trigger a gerrit-verify workflow_dispatch and confirm the Verified vote is cast in Gerrit.'
findings.finding: AC2 asserts 'main + PR triggers unchanged; gerrit-verify Verified path unaffected' but provides no end-state-observable verification step for either sub-claim, leaving both non-deferred goals without a faithful proof.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'main + PR triggers unchanged; gerrit-verify Verified path unaffected'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC2 to: 'main + PR triggers unchanged — evidence: push a commit to main and confirm gh run list --branch main shows test/eval/optionality runs as before; gerrit-verify Verified path unaffected — evidence: trigger gerrit-verify workflow_dispatch on a feature branch and confirm Verified vote appears in Gerrit.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before merging, verify and document (e.g., in the PR description or a repo comment) that refs/heads/feature/** replication to GitHub is actively configured — cite the specific g2p/Gerrit replication config entry or SSM parameter — so the plan's stated motivation can be confirmed against a real codebase artifact.
findings.finding: The plan's core premise — that epic 88ab's replication configuration pushes refs/heads/feature/** to the GitHub mirror, causing push-triggered CI fan-out — is an assertion about Gerrit/g2p infrastructure config that cannot be verified from any file in the repo. No replication config, g2p ini, or Gerrit project.config entry confirming `refs/heads/feature/**` replication to GitHub exists in the codebase. The 88ab ticket's own comments note the g2p artifact is `gerrit_to_platform.ini.template` rendered at boot from SSM, and that S3 (will-tile-plum 2e8a) has not yet verified branch-dispatch behavior. If replication of feature/** to GitHub is not actually active, the entire motivation for this plan is moot.
findings.location: Why section / premise: 'The epic 88ab feature-branch flow (S3) replicates refs/heads/feature/** (all nesting depths) to the GitHub mirror'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
